title: The First Question
description: A lost record of isolated knowledge: one question, four choices, instant feedback, and a short explanation.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: At first, the world was only one question.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: But one question was enough to prove that the world could answer.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A learner could choose from four paths.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: The record could answer correct or wrong, then explain why.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: One question worked... so the next question was how to organize many.

projectStage.title: Record I: The Lone Question
projectStage.caption: A recovered memory from the world before towers: one question, four choices, feedback, and explanation.

title: The First Families
description: Knowledge was gathered into families, so each path could be explored with purpose.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: One question became many.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: But many questions without order would become noise.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: So we gathered them into families.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Each family became a path of learning.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The paths were clear... but paths asked for a place.

projectStage.title: Record II: The First Ordering
projectStage.caption: A recovered memory from the first ordering of knowledge, when scattered questions found their families.

title: The First Map
description: The first towers appeared, turning paths of knowledge into places explorers could visit.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Paths asked for a place.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: So we drew the first map.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Each knowledge family became a tower on the island.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Explorers no longer followed a list. They traveled through a world.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A map is only a picture until it has rules.

projectStage.title: Record III: The First Map
projectStage.caption: A living fragment from the age when towers first rose across the island.

title: The First Rules
description: The world gained rules: challenges, progress, key fragments, and consequences.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A map is only a picture until it has rules.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: So towers became challenges.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Correct answers restored energy. Wrong answers lowered HP.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Key fragments marked progress, and the center stayed locked.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The world could now be played... but a journey can be lost if the world cannot remember.

projectStage.title: Record IV: The Forging of Rules
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the age when HP, combos, Seal Energy, clearing, and failure were forged into law.

title: The First Memory
description: The world learned to preserve memory and return to remembered paths.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A journey can be lost if the world cannot remember.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: So we gave the world memory.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Memory wrote progress into a record.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: The remembered path could be restored.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: With memory restored, the pieces could become a full journey.

projectStage.title: Record V: The Preserved Memory
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the age when progress could be preserved as a record and restored later.

title: The First Journey
description: The fragments became a complete world with an opening path, guides, Chronicles, Time Fragments, final trial, and ending.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: At last, the pieces became a journey.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: There was a beginning, a guide, records, fragments, trials, and an ending.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Students could enter as a team and meet Sage Tong.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: They could recover Chronicles, witness Time Fragments, and face the Creator's Trial.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The world was complete... but no one had yet walked its center path.

projectStage.title: Record VI: The Complete World
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the age when the scattered pieces became one complete world.
